bidirectional_reconstructions.from_rccd: RCCD plus finite domain signatures, rule schemas, and interpretation parameters reconstructs FARA without a new kernel commitment
bidirectional_reconstructions.to_rccd: FARA recovers R1-R5 through explicit representations, admissibility rules, dependency calculus, history, and bounded query operations

bidirectional_reconstructions.from_rccd: RCCD plus transition labeling, provenance schema, and observation parameters reconstructs LTS-PROV without a new kernel commitment
bidirectional_reconstructions.to_rccd: LTS-PROV recovers R1-R5 through labeled state commitments, admissibility, provenance-sensitive revision, path identity, and effective queries

bidirectional_reconstructions.from_rccd: RCCD plus functor, observation algebra, and dynamics parameters reconstructs the coalgebraic realization without a new kernel commitment
bidirectional_reconstructions.to_rccd: COALG-DYN recovers R1-R5 through observable commitments, constrained coalgebraic evolution, dependency annotations, behavioral history, and bounded observations

commitment_equivalence_classes.differences: representation form and domain parameters only within the registered C/P/E scope

additional_essential_commitment_search.reason: each candidate is domain-specific, derivable, or absent from at least one faithful realization

rccd_reducibility.basis: W7 supplies a separating lower bound for each R1-R5 component; removal of any component violates at least one P obligation
rccd_reducibility.scope_limit: relative to C, P, and E only

incomparable_kernel_search.note: this does not quantify over every logically possible future architecture

pareto_frontier.interpretation: the three formerly incomparable architectures are incomparable implementations inside one commitment-equivalence class, not distinct necessary kernels

nonclaims: unrestricted universality over all possible reasoning
